Azole antifungal. First-line for vulvovaginal candidiasis (VVC), oral/esophageal candidiasis, candidemia. 200mg dose for esophageal candidiasis or maintenance therapy. VVC maintenance: 200mg weekly. Contraindicated pregnancy first trimester. Check liver function if prolonged use.

Artemisinin derivative for SEVERE/complicated malaria. IM injection for cerebral malaria, severe anemia (Hgb <5g/dL), renal failure, acidosis. 150mg daily IM x 3 days. Follow with complete ACT course. NOT first-line (prefer artesunate IV). Hospital use only.

Co-amoxiclav 457mg/5mL = Amoxicillin 400mg + Clavulanic acid 57mg per 5mL. Treats resistant URTI, otitis media, sinusitis, pneumonia, UTI. Dose: 25-45mg/kg/day divided BID. Refrigerate after reconstitution, use within 10 days. Common diarrhea side effect.

Topical neuropathic pain cream. Contains: Gabapentin 6%, Ketoprofen 10%, Lidocaine 5%, Capsaicin 0.025%. Treats diabetic neuropathy, post-herpetic neuralgia, musculoskeletal pain. Apply thin layer TID-QID. Avoid broken skin, eyes. Wash hands after application.

Triple antimicrobial: Betamethasone 0.05% + Clotrimazole 1% + Neomycin/Gentamicin. Treats infected fungal dermatitis, eczema with secondary infection, intertrigo. Apply thin layer BID x 7-14 days max. DO NOT use on face. NOT for viral infections. Prolonged steroid causes skin thinning.

Generic throat lozenges. Medicated: benzocaine/lidocaine 5-10mg, menthol, antibacterial. Treats sore throat, pharyngitis, cough. Dissolve slowly q2-3h, max 8-12/day. NOT for children <4 years (choking risk). Symptomatic relief only - does NOT treat infection.

DHA-PPQ is WHO-recommended ACT for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. Adult dose: 3 tablets once daily x 3 days. Weight-based pediatric dosing. Alternative to AL. Longer post-treatment prophylaxis (piperaquine 14-28 days). QTc prolongation risk - avoid with other QT drugs.
